TLS meeting

Ada, Hanna, Zhihan
26.08.2025

#1 H&E analysis of TLS

Hanna’s progress

Cohort

Malignant tumor samples:
> 446 tumors from 343 patients

Benign omentum samples:
> 20 tumors from 15 patients
> 6 benign + 14 borderlines

Visual assessment of MS, Lagg, iTLS, and mTLS

→ 22 tumors

Range 0-20 / tumor
(4 with > 2 mTLS)

→ 64 tumors

Range 0-8 / tumor
(15 with > 2 iTLS)

→ 201 tumors

Range 0-112 / tumor
(120 with > 2 Lagg)

Ben/bord omentums:
9 Lagg+ tumors of which 5 have also iTLS/mTLS

Range: 0-67 Lagg
0-18 iTLS
0-12 mTLS

Questions related to cell segmentation

How well can discriminate macrophages and inflammatory cells?

How well works for for thick, ruptured, imperfectly-stained samples?

→ Validation for some samples by comparing with already-stained CycIF samples
→ Dropping out some poor samples?

SPACEstat: Defining TLS

Size/cell count-based classification
TLS: immature TLS (20-300 nuclei), intermediate TLS (300-500 nuclei), mature TLS (500+ nuclei) (MacFawn 2024)

Cell count, type and proximity-based classification
Lagg: epithelial enrichment score <0.8, combined T and B cell enrichment scores >1, clusters must include >50 cells, cells must be within 15 µm of each other (Xu 2024)

Cell count, type and zoning (Kroeger 2016)
type I: 20-50 cells, T and B cells, occasionally DCs > “small Lagg” 
type II: 100+ cells, T and B cells, lacked discrete zones or follicles > “large Lagg”
type III: B cell follicles with GC-like structures, discrete T cell zone, DCs and HEV-like vessels > “mature TLS”
type IV: 100-300 B cells, DC follicles, few T cells > as primarily found in omentum, these were speculated to be mostly normal milky spots

Milky spots: 300-750 µm diameter (Liu 2016, Okabe 2024)

Samples to CycIF: Lagg vs. iTLS vs. mTLS

Tumors with Lagg, iTLS, and mTLS
→ Differences between Lagg and TLS (size, cell density, cell composition, cell zoning)

Potential sample count:
12 tumors with Lagg, iTLS and mTLS
5 tumors with 2 or more of each maturation stages/tumor

Samples to CycIF: TLS+ vs. TLS- tumors

Lagg+/TLS+ tumors vs. Lagg+/TLS- tumors
→ Differences between Lagg metrics/cell composition between TLS+ and TLS- tumors? Which Laggs potentially develop into TLS, why some won’t?
→ Differences in clinical and molecular parameters, overall immune landscape? What factors affect on TLS development from Lagg?

Potential sample count:
Lagg+/TLS+: 19 tumors
Lagg+/TLS-: 3 tumors

Samples to CycIF: Milky spots vs. Lagg

MS+ ben/bord omentum vs. Lagg+ omentum (both PDS)
Laggs located in stroma, (interface), and intratumor
→ Ben/bord Laggs vs. tumor Laggs: are milky spots different?
→ Stromal vs. intratumoral Lagg: any differences, why some are capable to invade tumor?

Potential sample count:
MS+ ben/bord omentum: 9 tumors
PDS-treated, omentum tumors with both stromal and intratumoral Lagg: 11 tumors (9 tumors with also interface Lagg)

Samples to CycIF: mTLS in malignants vs. non-malignants

mTLS+ ben/bord omentum vs. mTLS omentum (both PDS)
mTLS located in stroma, (interface), and intratumor
→ Ben/bord mTLS vs. tumor mTLS: are mTLS different?
→ Stromal vs. intratumoral mTLS: any differences, why some are capable to invade tumor?

Potential sample count:
mTLS+ ben/bord omentum: 4 tumors
PDS-treated, omentum tumors with both stromal and intratumoral mTLS: 1 (has also interface mTLS)

Open questions

What markers to CycIF?
How many samples in total and how many samples from each comparison are needed and reasonable to stain?
How to discriminate active TLS from inactive, some additional method after CycIF? Proteomics/BCR sequencing?

#2 - t-CycIF advances

Ada’s progress (June-Aug-2025)

t-CycIF progress

Preprocessing + QC + Normalizations + Phenotyping

Final remarks

New antibody assessment needs to be performed before deep-phenotyping
Should we add more phenotypes?
All this can be found documented and explained in detail in the proj.log

#3 - GeoMx analysis

Zhihan’s progress

TLS AOIs - tSNE+UMAP - Patient

Quality issue - Low GDR for minibatch 1, 3 and 4

Chemokines/receptors: CXCL13, CCL19, CCL20, CCL21, CXCR5, CXCR3, CCR7
TLS signaling: LTA, LTB, LTBR, ​​TNFSF13B (BAFF), TNFRSF13C (BAFFR), LAMP3
B cells/germinal centers: MS4A1 (CD20), CD79A, CD19, BCL6, CD22, CD27
T cells/Tfh pathways: ICOS, PDCD1 (PD-1), IL21, SATB1, CD3D, CD3E, CD3G, CD4, CD8A, CD8B, CD28, CTLA4, IL2RA (CD25), FOXP3, LAG3, TIGIT
Plasma cell differentiation: MZB1, XBP1, PRDM1, IGHM, CD38, CD138
Cytokines - T: IFNG, IL2, IL4, IL17A, IL21; 
Cytokines - B: IL6, IL10

Key TLS genes boxplot

DEG - S

Functional Pathway Enrichment - S

Next steps for GeoMx analysis

Normalization + Remove Batch effect for only TLS AOIs
Check quality issues / re-QC / resequencing to recover more TLS AOIs data
Deconvolution analysis - TLS cell franctions distributions
Find and check interesting TLS related pathways, key genes / related functions inside
Spatial pattern recognition
Cell-cell interactions (ligand–receptor analysis)
Statistical analysis
